abstract: Creates a Live Photo editing context for the specified editing input.
overview:
## Return Value

A new Live Photo editing context, or `nil` if the provided content editing input does not represent a Live Photo.

## Discussion

In an app using the Photos framework, you obtain a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingInput] object by calling [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHAsset/requestContentEditingInput(with:completionHandler:)] method of a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHAsset] object that you’ve previously fetched.

In a photo editing extension that runs within the Photos app, your extension’s main view controller (which adopts the [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/PhotosUI/PHContentEditingController] protocol) receives a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingInput] object when the user chooses to edit a Live Photo with your extension.

You can create a Live Photo editing context only from [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingInput] object that represents a Live Photo. Use the [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingInput/livePhoto] property of the editing input to verify that it has live Photo content.

# init(livePhotoEditingInput:)

**Initializer**

Creates a Live Photo editing context for the specified editing input.

## Declaration

```swift
init?(livePhotoEditingInput livePhotoInput: PHContentEditingInput)
```

## Parameters

- **livePhotoInput**: A content editing input object representing the Live Photo for which to perform editing.

## Return Value

A new Live Photo editing context, or `nil` if the provided content editing input does not represent a Live Photo.

## Discussion

In an app using the Photos framework, you obtain a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingInput] object by calling [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHAsset/requestContentEditingInput(with:completionHandler:)] method of a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHAsset] object that you’ve previously fetched.

In a photo editing extension that runs within the Photos app, your extension’s main view controller (which adopts the [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/PhotosUI/PHContentEditingController] protocol) receives a [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingInput] object when the user chooses to edit a Live Photo with your extension.

You can create a Live Photo editing context only from [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingInput] object that represents a Live Photo. Use the [doc://com.apple.photokit/documentation/Photos/PHContentEditingInput/livePhoto] property of the editing input to verify that it has live Photo content.
